description: city in Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Izu (伊豆市, Izu-shi; Japanese pronunciation: [i.(d)zɯ, i.(d)zɯꜜ.ɕi]) is a city located in central Izu Peninsula in Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan.

description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Shizuoka Prefecture (静岡県, Shizuoka-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [ɕi.(d)zɯꜜ.o.ka, ɕi.(d)zɯ.o.kaꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Chūbu region of Honshu.

surface_forms: the war between Russia and Japan
description: conflict between the Russian and Japanese empires from 1904 to 1905
description_wiki: The Russo-Japanese War (8 February 1904 – 5 September 1905) was fought between the Russian Empire and the Empire of Japan over rival imperial ambitions in Manchuria and the Korean Empire.

surface_forms: the peace treaty was signed in Newport, Rhode Island
description: 1905 peace treaty that ended the Russo-Japanese War
description_wiki: The Treaty of Portsmouth formally ended the 1904–1905 Russo-Japanese War.

surface_forms: the president of the U.S. was Roosevelt
description: 26th President of the United States from 1901 to 1909 (1858–1919)
description_wiki: Theodore Roosevelt Jr.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: private research university located in Pasadena, California, USA
description_wiki: The California Institute of Technology (branded as Caltech) is a private research university/institute of technology in Pasadena, California, United States.

description: public high school in Pasadena, California
description_wiki: Pasadena High School (PHS) is a public high school in Pasadena, California.

description: island country in East Asia
description_wiki: Japan is an island country in East Asia.

description: city in Shizuoka prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Shimoda (下田市, Shimoda-shi) is a city and port located in Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan.

description: private university in Tokyo, Japan
description_wiki: Keio University (Japanese: 慶應義塾大学, Hepburn: Keiō Gijuku Daigaku), abbreviated as Keio (慶應) or Keidai (慶大), is a private research university located in Minato, Tokyo, Japan.

description: designated city in Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Yokohama (Japanese: 横浜; pronounced [jokohama] ) is the second-largest city in Japan by population as well as by area, and the country's most populous municipality.

description: ship built in 1972
state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Hawaii (  hə-WY-ee; Hawaiian: Hawaiʻi [həˈvɐjʔi, həˈwɐjʔi]) is an island state of the United States, in the Pacific Ocean about 2,000 miles (3,200 km) southwest of the U.S. mainland.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: historic hotel in San Francisco, California, United States
description_wiki: The Fairmont San Francisco is a luxury hotel at 950 Mason Street, atop Nob Hill in San Francisco, California.

aliases: Presbyterian Church in the United States
United Presbyterian Church in the U.S.A.
description: Mainline Protestant denomination in the United States
description_wiki: The Presbyterian Church (USA), abbreviated PCUSA, is a mainline Protestant denomination in the United States.

description: public community college in Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: Los Angeles City College (LACC) is a public community college in East Hollywood, California.

description: American newspaper (1903–1962)
description_wiki: The Los Angeles Examiner was a newspaper founded in 1903 by William Randolph Hearst  in Los Angeles.

description: city in Los Angeles County, California, United States
description_wiki: Pasadena (  PAS-ə-DEE-nə) is a city in Los Angeles County, California, United States, 10 miles (16 km) northeast of downtown Los Angeles.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.
